known_drug_rows.notes: Clinically used influenza antiviral; use as NA positive control in A/H1N1 workflow
Clinically used NA inhibitor; useful as polar active-site reference
Clinically used IV NA inhibitor; useful as reference control
Used in some regions and useful as structural NA inhibitor reference

structure_rows.description: 2009 pandemic H1N1 neuraminidase complexed with oseltamivir
2009 pandemic H1N1 neuraminidase complexed with zanamivir
2009 pandemic H1N1 neuraminidase complexed with laninamivir

primary_structure.description: 2009 pandemic H1N1 neuraminidase complexed with oseltamivir

binding_site.description: Conserved neuraminidase active site around the co-crystallized inhibitor, with charged and polar contacts that recognize sialic-acid-like ligands.
binding_site.box_strategy: Use co-crystallized oseltamivir centroid from 3TI6 or a prepared receptor-ligand complex.

computational_validation: Dock generated molecules into 3TI6-derived NA active site.
Run PoseBusters on top docked poses.
Compare generated molecules against controls by rank, pose pass rate, and scaffold diversity.
experimental_validation: Neuraminidase inhibition assay is the most direct biochemical validation route.
Cell-based influenza replication assays are downstream confirmation, not first-line CLI validation.
closed_loop_assets.assay_path: Neuraminidase inhibition assay is the most direct biochemical validation route. | Cell-based influenza replication assays are downstream confirmation, not first-line CLI validation.

evidence_quality.interpretation: Evidence score ranks source readiness for the computational closed loop; it is not a measured efficacy score.
